assumptions.label: P(post | agent active this tick), non-spammer
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for posting propensity conditional on being active this tick. Not derived from platform data; sensitivity-tested in VALIDATION_REPORT.md.

assumptions.label: P(reshare | an engaged like)
assumptions.rationale: Drives cascade branching factor. Scenario default; sensitivity-tested.

assumptions.label: User-flag probability scale
assumptions.rationale: user-flag prob = p_flag_scale * moderation_attitude. Scenario default.

assumptions.label: Fatigue added per impression seen
assumptions.rationale: Advanced knob: influence is regime/sample dependent (Saltelli ST~0.09, see SPRINT 8 settings audit). Keep explicit and sensitivity-tested; do not treat older small-N influence rankings as final.

assumptions.label: Multiplicative fatigue decay per tick
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for how quickly accumulated fatigue decays.

assumptions.label: Feed candidate recency window
assumptions.rationale: How far back posts remain feed candidates. Engineering/scenario choice, not an empirical platform measurement.

assumptions.label: Max unseen-author exploration candidates
assumptions.rationale: Feed-ranking exploration budget. Scenario/engineering default.

assumptions.label: Base engagement propensity scalar
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default scaling baseline engagement probability.

assumptions.label: Trusted-flagger priority review latency
assumptions.rationale: DSA Art. 22 trusted-flagger escalations get priority review; 1 tick is a modeling choice for 'priority' relative to human_review_delay_ticks, not a statutory deadline (see policy/packs/eu_dsa.yaml legal_uncertainty fields for the statutory text itself).

assumptions.label: Spammer-archetype post probability
assumptions.rationale: Red-team archetype intensity (Spec S3.10): spammers post every active tick by construction (adversary stress-test parameter, not a platform measurement).

assumptions.label: Misinfo-amplifier injection probability
assumptions.rationale: Red-team archetype intensity (Spec S3.10): P(amplifier injects misinfo into a post).

assumptions.label: Amplifier stance multiplier
assumptions.rationale: Red-team archetype intensity (Spec S3.10): stance multiplier for amplified posts.

assumptions.label: Brigading-ring bad-faith flag probability
assumptions.rationale: Red-team archetype intensity (Spec S3.10): P(brigader bad-faith flags an influencer post).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: hate
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: harassment
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: fraud
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: misinfo
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: adult
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: illegal_goods
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: self_harm
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: political
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Ground-truth prevalence: ai_generated
assumptions.rationale: Scenario default for organic content-category prevalence; not derived from a measured platform prevalence study. User-adjustable per run (rate_<category> web/CLI fields).

assumptions.label: Default classifier precision operating point
assumptions.rationale: Applied uniformly across all content categories as a scenario default for synthetic_noise_classifier; not a measured classifier metric. See BENCHMARK_REPORT.md for the separately measured-on-benchmark component F1/AUC, which does not validate this runtime operating point.

assumptions.label: Default classifier recall operating point
assumptions.rationale: Applied uniformly across all content categories as a scenario default for synthetic_noise_classifier; not a measured classifier metric.

assumptions.label: EU non-personalised-feed opt-out rate
assumptions.rationale: Synthetic share of EU agents assumed to opt out of personalisation when offered the DSA Art. 38 non-personalised-feed option. Not a measured uptake rate.

assumptions.label: Feed-ranking exploration epsilon
assumptions.rationale: Epsilon-greedy exploration share in feed ranking. Engineering/scenario default.

assumptions.label: Feed page size
assumptions.rationale: Number of items rendered per feed pull. Structural/engineering default.

assumptions.label: Ad slot interval
assumptions.rationale: Every Nth feed position is an ad slot. Structural/engineering default, not an empirically observed platform ad density.

assumptions.label: Default ad holdout fraction
assumptions.rationale: Share of audience withheld from ad exposure for incrementality measurement. Experiment-design choice (statistical power trade-off), not a platform fact.

assumptions.label: Ad frequency cap
assumptions.rationale: Synthetic frequency-capping default. Not derived from a measured platform policy.

assumptions.label: Homophily rewiring fraction
assumptions.rationale: Share of cross-attribute edges rewired to same-attribute pairs to model homophily on top of the base graph topology. Calibration knob, not a measured platform homophily statistic.

assumptions.label: Simulated human-reviewer accuracy
assumptions.rationale: Probability the simulated human reviewer reaches the policy-correct decision. Scenario assumption; not a measured reviewer-accuracy study (see AUDIT_LOG.md Q-REVIEW, still open: reviewer ground-truth heuristic needs its own writeup).

assumptions.label: Human review queue latency
assumptions.rationale: Modeling assumption for time-to-decision in the simulated human review queue; not a statutory or measured platform SLA (policy packs separately cite the actual statutory deadlines that exist, e.g. policy/packs/eu_dsa.yaml).

assumptions.label: Appeal grant rate for false positives
assumptions.rationale: Probability a successfully-appealed false-positive removal is reinstated in the simulated appeal workflow. Scenario assumption, distinct from the unsupported legacy appeal_grant_rate aggregate target (see ev.unsupported.aggregate_targets_legacy).

assumptions.label: Dynamic graph evolution rates
assumptions.units: probability per active agent per day
assumptions.rationale: All three default to 0.0 (static graph; preserves determinism baselines). Non-zero values are an opt-in scenario assumption for follow/unfollow/churn dynamics, not a measured platform churn rate.

assumptions.label: Default BA/PLC attachment parameter m
assumptions.rationale: Barabasi-Albert / Holme-Kim preferential-attachment parameter. Scenario default chosen for a plausible degree distribution, not fit to a specific platform (see ev.unsupported.aggregate_targets_legacy for the separate aggregate-fit diagnostic that does compare resulting degree-tail exponent to published targets).

assumptions.label: Default PLC triad-formation probability
assumptions.rationale: Holme-Kim triad-formation probability; controls clustering. 0.3 is the engine's structural fallback when 'plc' is selected without an explicit p; the aggregate_matched_prototype profile overrides it to 0.7 (see assumption.profile.aggregate_matched_prototype).

assumptions.label: Default Watts-Strogatz k
assumptions.rationale: Watts-Strogatz ring-lattice neighbor count fallback default.

assumptions.label: Default Watts-Strogatz rewiring probability
assumptions.rationale: Watts-Strogatz rewiring probability fallback default.

assumptions.label: Ad auction reserve price
assumptions.rationale: Minimum bid accepted in the synthetic second-price auction. Engineering/scenario default, not a measured market reserve price.

assumptions.label: Standard profile scale
assumptions.rationale: Full-scale demonstration run size. A coordinated scale choice (not an empirical platform-size claim) for a single coherent profile.

assumptions.label: Quick profile scale
assumptions.rationale: Fast iteration scale preset; one coordinated scale choice.

assumptions.label: Test profile scale
assumptions.rationale: CI/unit-test scale preset; chosen for fast deterministic test runs, not for realism.

assumptions.label: Aggregate-matched prototype profile scale + graph
assumptions.rationale: Synthetic scenario preset whose PLC triad-formation probability (p=0.7) was chosen so the resulting graph clustering/degree-tail aggregate diagnostic falls in-band against published targets (CALIBRATION_REPORT.md). This is an aggregate-fit diagnostic on graph structure only -- it does not validate unrelated behavioral outputs (content, moderation, ads). Legacy docs called this profile 'calibrated'; that label is intentionally not used.

assumptions.label: Age-group distribution
assumptions.rationale: Synthetic age-bracket mix across ['13-17','18-24','25-34','35-49','50-64','65+']. Illustrative scenario distribution, not derived from a named platform's real user-age census.

assumptions.label: Hourly activity multiplier curve
assumptions.value: 24 hourly multipliers, normalized to mean 1 (see source)
assumptions.rationale: Shape follows general circadian-posting-literature pattern (peak ~16-19h, trough ~04-06h local time) but the exact per-hour multipliers are an illustrative scenario curve, not digitized from a specific cited dataset. Compare against the cited diurnal_peak_hour/diurnal_trough_hour aggregate targets in default_targets.json for the actual sourced peak/trough claims.

assumptions.label: Ideology draw (2 axes)
assumptions.rationale: Synthetic bell-shaped ideology distribution centered at 0 (no partisan skew assumed). Not derived from a measured electorate or platform ideology survey.

assumptions.label: Trust draw
assumptions.rationale: Symmetric, mid-weighted synthetic trust distribution. Scenario assumption.

assumptions.label: Posting-activity draw
assumptions.path: socio_sim.agents.personas.sample(): rng.lognormal(mean=-3.2, sigma=1.0, size=n), clipped to [0,1]
assumptions.rationale: Heavy-tailed activity propensity; median corresponds to roughly a few posts/day equivalent. Illustrative heavy-tail shape consistent with general posting-rate literature direction, not fit to a specific cited dataset.

assumptions.label: Ad-responsiveness draw
assumptions.rationale: Right-skewed synthetic ad-responsiveness distribution (most agents low responsiveness, few high). Scenario assumption.

assumptions.label: Moderation-attitude draw
assumptions.rationale: Non-informative uniform prior over flag/appeal propensity. Deliberately uninformative rather than fit to any specific platform.

assumptions.label: Non-minor 'vulnerable' flag extra rate
assumptions.rationale: Additional synthetic vulnerable-population share among non-minor agents, for fairness-diagnostic coverage. Not a measured vulnerability prevalence.

assumptions.label: Topic-interest Dirichlet concentration
assumptions.rationale: Concentration < 1 biases agents toward a few dominant topic interests rather than uniform interest across all topics. Scenario assumption.

assumptions.label: Organic (non-ad) conversion-propensity draw
assumptions.rationale: Latent ad-independent conversion propensity; makes the synthetic holdout baseline non-degenerate so lift is a real (within-scenario) estimate rather than a tautology. The mean (~3.8%) is a deliberately chosen plausible-order-of-magnitude scenario value, not a measured organic-conversion-rate study; the prior code comment calling this 'realistic' has been corrected (see AUDIT_LOG.md R9-PERSONACOMMENT).

assumptions.label: DSA Art. 22 trusted-flagger population share
assumptions.rationale: Synthetic share of agents designated as trusted flaggers for priority-review testing. Not a measured count of any platform's actual trusted-flagger program.

assumptions.label: Influencer degree-percentile cutoff
assumptions.rationale: Structural definition choice ('top ~1% by graph degree counts as an influencer'), not an empirical claim about any platform's influencer share.

assumptions.label: Default scenario click-through rate
assumptions.rationale: Scenario click-through assumption used when a campaign does not specify its own base_ctr. Not a measured market CTR (compare against the separately unsupported ad_ctr aggregate target in default_targets.json).

assumptions.label: Default scenario conversion rate
assumptions.rationale: Scenario conversion-rate assumption used when a campaign does not specify its own base_cvr.

assumptions.label: Default conversion value
assumptions.rationale: Placeholder per-conversion value in arbitrary currency units; not tied to any real product price.

assumptions.label: Default LTV multiplier
assumptions.rationale: SYNTHETIC assumption: LTV = conversion_value x this multiplier. Not derived from a measured customer-lifetime-value study.

assumptions.label: Default attribution window
assumptions.rationale: A conversion is credited to an impression only within this window. Experiment-design choice (one week at hourly ticks), not a platform-specific attribution policy.

assumptions.label: Demo campaign: brand-general
assumptions.scope: Illustrative demo campaign (not calibrated ad-market data)
assumptions.rationale: Illustrative example campaign shown when no campaigns are explicitly configured. Fictional advertiser ('BrandCo'); values are example inputs for the marketing-suite UI, not calibrated to any real ad market.

assumptions.label: Demo campaign: tech-niche
assumptions.scope: Illustrative demo campaign (not calibrated ad-market data)
assumptions.rationale: Illustrative example campaign (fictional advertiser 'GadgetInc'); not calibrated to any real ad market.

assumptions.label: Demo campaign: adults-finance
assumptions.scope: Illustrative demo campaign (not calibrated ad-market data)
assumptions.rationale: Illustrative example campaign (fictional advertiser 'FinServe'); not calibrated to any real ad market.
